scope_note: This pair covers ONLY onshore wind turbines under CONVECTIVE wind sub-perils: tornado [T] and straight-line/derecho wind [W]. The hazard axis is X_SPEED_RATIO_TO_IEC (r = V_3s_hub / Ve50_class, default IEC class II Ve50=59.5 m/s). Straight-line/derecho is the SLW (D50-baseline) pathway of the canonical logistic curves; tornado is the same curves shifted left by the per-unit tornado_D50_shift with a 0.10 core-exposure fraction. Explicitly OUT OF SCOPE (named-and-quarantined distinct perils): HURRICANE/TROPICAL-CYCLONE wind, and HAIL-dominant SCS. Offshore wind is out of scope.
golden_rule_on_grain: A source with a wind loss number is NOT a convective-wind benchmark until its sub-peril frame (tornado vs straight-line/derecho vs hurricane vs hail-SCS), denominator (nameplate MW AND struck-turbine count for tornado corridors), coverage, asset class, geography, and loss basis are understood. Every number carries a sub_peril_family tag so hurricane/hail-contaminated numbers are quarantined, not silently averaged. A farm-average $/MW is NOT grain-comparable to a per-struck-turbine tornado corridor loss.

upstream_cell_governance.hazard_axis: X_SPEED_RATIO_TO_IEC (r = V_3s_hub / Ve50_class; IEC I 70 / II 59.5 / III 52.5 m/s)

notes: wind_convective_wind crosswalk is RESOLVED_FROM_DAMAGE_MODELING: the canonical wind_tornado_wind cell is PROMOTED (canonical_runtime_artifact=true) so the 5 named WT_* failure units, their value shares, the speed-ratio hazard axis, the logistic curve form, and the speed-ratio->DR ordinates are all used verbatim / computed from the canonical logistic parameters. This is a full Case A resolution, not PENDING and not DR-withheld.

The convective-wind failure grain is 5 STRUCTURAL/consequential units (blade, tower, nacelle, foundation; plus secondary power-electronics open-seam), unlike flood (~8 electrical units) or hail (one PV-module unit). NACELLE dominates the value ladder at 28.45% installed TIV (drivetrain is the costliest subsystem). Primary structural aggregate (4 default-agg units) = $1,215,627/MW = 61.77% installed TIV; all-5 = $1,275,678/MW = 64.82%. Value ladder is NREL CWER 2024 (2023 USD), peril-agnostic.

0 of 27 benchmark rows (other than the 2 value-ladder denominator anchors) normalize to a $/MW basis; every normalized_usd_per_MW cell is blank (never zero). Isolated per-turbine convective-wind $/MW physical severity is genuinely absent from the open literature.

The strongest open dollar figure, Flat Ridge 2 v. Underwriters at Lloyd's (2012 KS tornado): premium $1.8M, claim submitted $9,728,755.38, adjuster estimate >$12M, paid $7.25M net of $250K deductible. It is WITHHELD from the $/MW axis because the primary court filing contains NO MW capacity and NO struck-turbine count; a tornado damages a narrow corridor, so a farm-average $/MW would understate per-struck-turbine severity.

The single best straight-line/derecho anchor is a MEASURED NULL: in the 2020 Midwest derecho, MidAmerican reported no significant wind-farm damage at ~126 mph gust (r well below the SLW D50); the Sept/Oct blade failures were LIGHTNING, not wind. This is the empirical low end of the SLW pathway and is recorded as a real zero (blank, not 0).

Bouchard & Romanic (2023) provides a peer-reviewed tornado fragility (logistic v_m=49.1 m/s, k=0.504) that corroborates the curve FORM but is German and NOT a US plug-in (CURVE_FORM_ANALOG). FEMA NRI keeps Tornado EAL ($146.6M Atlanta 11-county) and Strong-Wind EAL ($5.35M) separate, mirroring the [T]/[W] split, but is an annualized EAL (downstream, DIFFERENT_METRIC_FAMILY). Moody's +58% 'US wind PML' is hurricane-contaminated and QUARANTINED.

The wind_tornado_wind curve is a public-source-anchored engineering parameterization on a speed-ratio axis; the cell owns SEVERITY curves only (EAL/PML/VaR/TVaR are downstream in the hazard catalog / financial model).
Row counts above equal the actual data-row counts of the corresponding CSVs (benchmark=27, source_matrix=77, crosswalk=27, intensity=20) and the source_registry.json length (77).
